name: Smoked Gouda and Caramelized Onion Quesadillas
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt 2 tablespoons butter in a medium skillet over medium heat.
2. Add onion, brown sugar, and vinegar; sauté until the onion is golden brown, stirring frequently for about 25 minutes. Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature.
3. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
4. Sprinkle cheese over half of each tortilla, dividing equally.
5. Sprinkle prosciutto and caramelized onion over the cheese on each tortilla. Season with pepper.
6. Fold the other half of the tortilla over the cheese mixture.
7. Brush the tortillas with some melted butter.
8. Heat a heavy large skillet over medium-high heat. Working in batches, cook quesadillas just until brown spots appear, brushing the skillet with butter between batches for about 2 minutes per side. Transfer cooked quesadillas to a heavy baking sheet.
9. Bake until the tortillas are golden and the cheese melts for about 5 minutes.
10. Transfer quesadillas to a serving platter. Cut each into 6 triangles and serve hot.
NOTE: I sometimes speed up the process by placing prepared quesadillas directly under the broiler for a quick minute or so on each side.
